2078-## Minimum Manual Checklist
2079-
--
2104-
2105-Meaning:
2106-
2107:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
2108-- `ACCEPTED`: the bounded step is accepted after human review.
2109-- `REJECTED`: the run is not accepted for progression, whether because execution failed, the result is inadequate, or the outcome requires redesign or retry analysis first.
2110-
--
2116-
2117-Rules:
2118-
2119:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
2120-- `ACCEPTED` releases exactly the next reviewed step.
2121:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
2122-
2123-`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
2124-`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
2125-
2126-## Stop-And-Decide Rule
2127-
2128:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
2129-
2130-That decision is intentionally outside this gate.
2131-It may result in:
--
2200-
2201-## Summary
2202-
2203:`tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md` is directionally aligned with the governing workflow because it pushes toward smaller parts, clearer ownership, and conservative growth. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` is acceptable as thin bridge tooling for a single bounded slice, but the current architecture doc does not yet translate the workflow into a clear V1 boundary, validation plan, review loop, or failure/resource evidence model. The next step should stay at the design layer: define the minimum execution-record artifact that every run must produce before more tooling is added.
2204-
2205-## Architecture Alignment Findings
2206-
--
2208-
2209-- The architecture doc is explicitly conservative, small-part oriented, and separation-of-concerns driven, which matches design-before-build and boundaries-before-breadth in the workflow ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:92), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:5)).
2210-- Its decomposition of the original script into repo paths, prompt parsing, note handling, status reconstruction, retry handling, and CLI concerns is a good responsibility split and a useful architecture starting point ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:177), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:33)).
2211:- It repeatedly says not to build everything at once and proposes a smaller folder shape before a larger one, which is consistent with thin slices before large pushes ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:100), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:121), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:246)).
2212-
2213-### Stale, incomplete, or misaligned
2214-
2215-- The architecture doc does not yet reflect the workflow's full sequence. It jumps from decomposition into a wish list and folder layout, but it does not define a short problem statement, explicit scope fence, out-of-scope list, minimum viable slice, artifact inventory, implementation order, or validation checklist as the governing workflow expects ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:141), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:159), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:196), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:214), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:233), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:251)).
2216-- The "eventually" section is useful as an extension path, but it is carrying too much conceptual breadth for a workflow that emphasizes bounded next steps. There is no explicit line saying which of those items are intentionally deferred from V1 ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:96), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:374), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:119)).
2217-- The document mentions logging and diagnostics, richer notes, retry intelligence, approval gates, and dependencies, but it does not specify the source of truth or the stable identity of important records beyond prompt stems. That leaves the durable-history model underdesigned relative to the workflow's emphasis on preserved local memory and stable identities ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:120), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:374), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:187)).
2218:- Review points between iterations are largely absent. The architecture doc talks about manual status marking and approval gates eventually, but not about the concrete review stop that should happen after each bounded run before the next run proceeds ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:262), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:208)).
2219-
2220-## Runner Alignment Findings
2221-
2222:### Useful and appropriately thin
2223-
2224:- The runner does one bounded thing: resolve a prompt, execute `codex exec`, and write a local note. That fits the workflow's allowance for thin bridge tooling that gathers evidence while remaining inspectable and reversible ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:114), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:99)).
2225:- It validates basic directory prerequisites and fails cleanly when expected folders or prompt files are missing, which is a reasonable minimal safeguard for a bridge script ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:109), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:108)).
2226:- It writes timestamped markdown notes into `notes/`, which gives the system at least some durable local history of what was run and what Codex returned ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:120), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:46), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:135)).
2227-
2228-### Responsibilities it is carrying that should move elsewhere later
2229-
2230:- Prompt resolution policy is embedded in the runner. Selection by absolute path, repo-relative path, `codex_prompts/` path, or globbed prefix is helpful now, but prompt identity and lookup rules belong in a prompt-discovery/status layer if the system grows ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:47), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:25)).
2231:- Note-path and note-content policy are also embedded here. That is acceptable for a bridge script, but note schema, status vocabulary, and execution-record structure should eventually be owned by a note/history module rather than the process launcher ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:96), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:187), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:50)).
2232:- The runner currently decides final state as `SUCCESS` or `FAILED` based only on the subprocess exit code. That is an expedient bridge behavior, but review judgment, validation outcome, and richer state modeling should not stay inside the launcher if the workflow matures ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:141), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:124)).
2233-
2234-### Current misalignment
2235-
2236:- As bridge tooling it is still acceptable, but it is only partially subordinate to workflow. It captures execution, yet it does not encode the review point that should separate execution from acceptance. A zero exit code becomes `SUCCESS` immediately, which skips the workflow's "validate and inspect before the next step" posture ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:271), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:125)).
2237:- It stores the original prompt text and final output, but not explicit success criteria, validation evidence, or reviewer judgment. That means the note is an execution artifact, not yet a full workflow artifact ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:251), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
2238-
2239-## Failure-Analysis Findings
2240-
2241:- The current setup provides some raw failure evidence because nonzero exit runs still write a note and stderr is preserved when present ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:343), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:128)).
2242-- That said, it does not support useful post-failure analysis very well. The note structure has no fields for failure classification, whether the failure was task/design/environment related, whether the task was too large, what changed on retry, or what the recommended next action is. The workflow explicitly wants failure to produce analysis rather than mere retry cost ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:125), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:343)).
2243-- The smallest improvement here is not a larger retry engine. It is a defined execution-note schema with a short "failure analysis" block for failed runs, even if the user fills some of it manually. That preserves inspectable local history without expanding the runner's responsibilities much.
2244-
2245-## Resource-Awareness Findings
2246-
2247:- The setup preserves only minimal cost evidence today: timestamp in the note filename/content and the captured final output/stderr. That gives some weak signal about artifact volume, but almost nothing about runtime, retries, or service usage ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:382), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:17), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
2248-- There is no recorded elapsed time, no attempt count or retry linkage, no output-size metric, and no token usage capture if Codex exposes it. The workflow asks for lightweight observation, not a billing system, and the current setup falls short of even that lightweight bar ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:130), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:382)).
2249-- The smallest improvement is to define a tiny metrics block in the execution record. Start with fields the runner can always capture cheaply: start time, end time, elapsed seconds, subprocess return code, stderr present yes/no, and output character count. Token usage can remain optional when available later.
2250-
--
2252-
2253-Create a short design artifact that defines the V1 execution record for one prompt run.
2254-
2255:That artifact should do four things only:
2256-
2257-1. Declare the source of truth for a run record.
2258-2. Define the minimum fields for execution, review, failure analysis, and lightweight metrics.
--

2427-notes/005_prompt_queue_plan__20260415_202557.md:19:This reduces the risk of hard-coding the wrong note model or the wrong queue semantics into the runner before the manual review loop is settled.
2428-notes/005_prompt_queue_plan__20260415_202557.md:37:The following work is intentionally not in this queue:
2429-tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:48:* one manual review gate
2430:tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:78:* stopping the queue at `UNREVIEWED`
2431-tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:91:* richer queue state machines
2432-tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:141:### 4. V1 review gate definition
2433-tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:155:No additional required queue file, database, or sidecar should be introduced in V1.
2434:tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:161:* `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` for bounded execution and execution-record creation
2435-tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:163:* `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` for conservative next-prompt readiness checks
2436-tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:185:* a generalized queue engine
2437-tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:223:The review gate is the minimum manual checkpoint after execution record creation.
2438-tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:234:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
2439-tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:245:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.
2440-tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:358:### 5. Conservative queue progression
2441:tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:368:* `run_prompt.py`
2442-tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:370:* `check_queue_readiness.py`

2444-tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:379:1. define the V1 review gate
--
2448-tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:459:* aggregated queue reports
2449-tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:481:2. keep the review gate explicit
2450-tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:484:5. defer broader queue features until the single-run slice has been used enough to reveal real needs
2451:notes/010_next_step_recommendation__20260415_234559.md:9:A short diagnosis artifact that identifies where session startup is trying to write, whether the failure reproduces independently of `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, and one bounded follow-up action.
2452-notes/017_queue_readiness_gap_explanation_polish__20260416_004458.md:5:Updated the human-readable stdout of `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` for the default-target case where missing earlier V1 execution-record history can make the result look surprising.
2453-notes/017_queue_readiness_gap_explanation_polish__20260416_004458.md:12:- those missing-V1 prompts also have legacy `__SUCCESS__` notes that a human might otherwise mistake for queue history
2454-notes/017_queue_readiness_gap_explanation_polish__20260416_004458.md:23:- legacy `__SUCCESS__` notes do not count as V1 queue history

2458-notes/017_queue_readiness_gap_explanation_polish__20260416_004458.md:47:   `Queue note: default selection uses only V1 execution records in notes/. Legacy __SUCCESS__ notes do not count as V1 queue history, so prompts 002, 003, 004, 005, 006, 007, 008, 009, 010 still count as missing V1 evidence and can pull the default target earlier than older notes suggest.`

2491-notes/016_queue_and_backlog_helper_validation__20260416_003710.md:5:I validated `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` and `tools/codex/list_review_backlog.py` against the current prompt order in `codex_prompts/` and the current V1 execution records in `notes/`.
2492-notes/016_queue_and_backlog_helper_validation__20260416_003710.md:7:Both helpers behaved conservatively and consistently with the V1 design documents. The main visible usability issue is that the default queue-readiness result points back to prompt `002`, because prompts `002` through `010` do not have V1 execution records even though later prompts `011` through `015` do.
--
2501-notes/016_queue_and_backlog_helper_validation__20260416_003710.md:88:The single smallest next improvement is to make `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` print a short explanation when the default target is blocked by a missing V1 history gap, especially in a repo like this one where prompts `002` through `010` have legacy notes but no V1 execution records.

2542:notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:967:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py" in /data/git/handson-ml3
2543:notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1129:`tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md` is directionally aligned with the governing workflow because it pushes toward smaller parts, clearer ownership, and conservative growth. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` is acceptable as thin bridge tooling for a single bounded slice, but the current architecture doc does not yet translate the workflow into a clear V1 boundary, validation plan, review loop, or failure/resource evidence model. The next step should stay at the design layer: define the minimum execution-record artifact that every run must produce before more tooling is added.
2544:notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1150:- The runner does one bounded thing: resolve a prompt, execute `codex exec`, and write a local note. That fits the workflow's allowance for thin bridge tooling that gathers evidence while remaining inspectable and reversible ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:114), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:99)).
2545:notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1151:- It validates basic directory prerequisites and fails cleanly when expected folders or prompt files are missing, which is a reasonable minimal safeguard for a bridge script ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:109), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:108)).
2546:notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1152:- It writes timestamped markdown notes into `notes/`, which gives the system at least some durable local history of what was run and what Codex returned ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:120), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:46), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:135)).
2547:notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1156:- Prompt resolution policy is embedded in the runner. Selection by absolute path, repo-relative path, `codex_prompts/` path, or globbed prefix is helpful now, but prompt identity and lookup rules belong in a prompt-discovery/status layer if the system grows ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:47), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:25)).
2548:notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1157:- Note-path and note-content policy are also embedded here. That is acceptable for a bridge script, but note schema, status vocabulary, and execution-record structure should eventually be owned by a note/history module rather than the process launcher ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:96), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:187), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:50)).
2549:notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1158:- The runner currently decides final state as `SUCCESS` or `FAILED` based only on the subprocess exit code. That is an expedient bridge behavior, but review judgment, validation outcome, and richer state modeling should not stay inside the launcher if the workflow matures ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:141), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:124)).
2550:notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1162:- As bridge tooling it is still acceptable, but it is only partially subordinate to workflow. It captures execution, yet it does not encode the review point that should separate execution from acceptance. A zero exit code becomes `SUCCESS` immediately, which skips the workflow's "validate and inspect before the next step" posture ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:271), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:125)).
2551:notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1163:- It stores the original prompt text and final output, but not explicit success criteria, validation evidence, or reviewer judgment. That means the note is an execution artifact, not yet a full workflow artifact ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:251), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
2552:notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1167:- The current setup provides some raw failure evidence because nonzero exit runs still write a note and stderr is preserved when present ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:343), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:128)).
2553:notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1173:- The setup preserves only minimal cost evidence today: timestamp in the note filename/content and the captured final output/stderr. That gives some weak signal about artifact volume, but almost nothing about runtime, retries, or service usage ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:382), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:17), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).

2634:notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:5:The V1 prompt workflow is now real, not just specified. The repo has a working runner at `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, an in-place review write-back helper at `tools/codex/review_run.py`, a queue-readiness checker at `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`, and a review-backlog lister at `tools/codex/list_review_backlog.py`.
2635:notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:13:- One bounded prompt run can be executed through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`.
2636-notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:17:- Conservative queue progression is operational through `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`.
2637:notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:31:  - It still targets `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`.
2638:notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:32:  - It still names the runner field as `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`.
2639-notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:33:  - It still frames review write-back and queue logic as not yet implemented, while the repo now has separate helpers for both.
2640:notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:34:- The execution-record spec says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` now adds same-second suffixes like `__2` when needed. The code is pragmatic; the spec lags.
2641:notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:42:The repo has outgrown parts of the design packet. The next prompt or reviewer could still be steered by stale references to `baby_run_prompt.py`, by the old strict run-id rule, or by the older assumption that readiness/backlog support does not exist.
2642:notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:52:`run_prompt.py`, `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py` all rely on the same markdown field and section conventions. That contract is now central to the workflow, but it is only protected by careful coding and prior notes, not by a small repeatable test layer.
2643-notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:55:- repeated required-section and required-field parsing logic in `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py`
2644-notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:65:That is not a V1 architecture failure, but it does create friction when interpreting readiness and when deciding whether to trust the queue as a day-to-day operational surface.
2645-notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:68:- `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` needed a special explanatory `Queue note:`
2646-notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:71:### 4. Defer retry-linkage tooling and richer queue semantics
2647-notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:100:4. `Retry-linkage helper or richer queue semantics`

2649:notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:9:- execute one prompt through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
2650:notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:20:- Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
2651:notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:21:- Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
2652:notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:22:- Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
2653:notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:23:- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
2654:notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:24:- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).
2655-notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:31:- The helper is consistent with the V1 review gate. It only allows `ACCEPTED` or `REJECTED`, auto-fills `reviewed_at_utc`, and limits failure-analysis updates to rejected runs. See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).

2763-notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:820:- it can be created cheaply by the bridge runner
2764-notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1252:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
2765:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1258:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
2766-notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1275:- a larger queue state machine
2767:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1321:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
2768:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1333:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
2769:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1335:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
2770-notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1337:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
2771-notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1338:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
2772:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1342:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
2773-notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1353:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
2774-notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1383:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
2775-notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1390:- automatic queue release
2776-notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1392:- separate queue-level state files
2777-notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1400:The minimum V1 review gate is:

2781:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1866:- Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
2782:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1867:- Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
2783:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1868:- Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
2784:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1869:- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
2785:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1870:- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).
2786-notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1877:- The helper is consistent with the V1 review gate. It only allows `ACCEPTED` or `REJECTED`, auto-fills `reviewed_at_utc`, and limits failure-analysis updates to rejected runs. See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).

2844:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:2806:The review concludes the V1 pipe is now a real usable thin slice: `run_prompt.py` creates durable execution records, `review_run.py` performs in-place manual review write-back, and the main remaining weakness is operational support for repeated use rather than missing architecture. The top recommendation is to add a small queue-readiness checker next, followed by an unreviewed-run lister and then lightweight contract validation for the two V1 scripts.

3369:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:200:The review concludes the V1 pipe is now a real usable thin slice: `run_prompt.py` creates durable execution records, `review_run.py` performs in-place manual review write-back, and the main remaining weakness is operational support for repeated use rather than missing architecture. The top recommendation is to add a small queue-readiness checker next, followed by an unreviewed-run lister and then lightweight contract validation for the two V1 scripts.
3370:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:202:I did not modify `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`, `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, or `tools/codex/review_run.py`.

3386-notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1080:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
3387-notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1091:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.
3388-notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1127:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
3389:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1133:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
3390-notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1150:- a larger queue state machine
3391:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1196:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
3392:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1208:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
3393:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1210:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
3394-notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1212:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
3395-notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1213:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
3396:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1217:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
3397-notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1228:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
3398-notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1258:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
3399-notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1265:- automatic queue release
3400-notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1267:- separate queue-level state files

3413:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2146:Built `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` as a thin V1 runner candidate that resolves one prompt, runs `codex exec` from the repo root, captures `--output-last-message`, captures stderr and return code, writes one V1 execution-record markdown file into `notes/`, prints that path, and exits with the subprocess return code.
3414:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2148:## Preserved From `baby_run_prompt.py`

3419:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2223:`tools/codex/run_prompt.py` appears to satisfy the intended V1 bridge-runner role: it executes one prompt, writes one durable execution record in `notes/`, separates execution from review, and stops at `review_status: UNREVIEWED`. The sample record is structurally close to the V1 artifact shape and preserves the failure evidence that matters. The most important next issue is environment-related, not runner design or broader implementation.
3420:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2227:### Does `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` successfully implement the V1 design intent?

3489:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4732:+- Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
3490:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4733:+- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
3491:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4734:+- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).
3492-notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4741:+- The helper is consistent with the V1 review gate. It only allows `ACCEPTED` or `REJECTED`, auto-fills `reviewed_at_utc`, and limits failure-analysis updates to rejected runs. See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).

See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
3562:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5653:- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
3563:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5654:- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).
3564-notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5661:- The helper is consistent with the V1 review gate. It only allows `ACCEPTED` or `REJECTED`, auto-fills `reviewed_at_utc`, and limits failure-analysis updates to rejected runs. See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).

3618:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6430:The review concludes the V1 pipe is now a real usable thin slice: `run_prompt.py` creates durable execution records, `review_run.py` performs in-place manual review write-back, and the main remaining weakness is operational support for repeated use rather than missing architecture. The top recommendation is to add a small queue-readiness checker next, followed by an unreviewed-run lister and then lightweight contract validation for the two V1 scripts.

3660:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:572:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py" in /data/git/handson-ml3
3661:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:998:`tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md` is directionally aligned with the governing workflow because it pushes toward smaller parts, clearer ownership, and conservative growth. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` is acceptable as thin bridge tooling for a single bounded slice, but the current architecture doc does not yet translate the workflow into a clear V1 boundary, validation plan, review loop, or failure/resource evidence model. The next step should stay at the design layer: define the minimum execution-record artifact that every run must produce before more tooling is added.
3662:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1019:- The runner does one bounded thing: resolve a prompt, execute `codex exec`, and write a local note. That fits the workflow's allowance for thin bridge tooling that gathers evidence while remaining inspectable and reversible ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:114), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:99)).
3663:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1020:- It validates basic directory prerequisites and fails cleanly when expected folders or prompt files are missing, which is a reasonable minimal safeguard for a bridge script ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:109), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:108)).
3664:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1021:- It writes timestamped markdown notes into `notes/`, which gives the system at least some durable local history of what was run and what Codex returned ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:120), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:46), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:135)).
3665:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1025:- Prompt resolution policy is embedded in the runner. Selection by absolute path, repo-relative path, `codex_prompts/` path, or globbed prefix is helpful now, but prompt identity and lookup rules belong in a prompt-discovery/status layer if the system grows ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:47), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:25)).
3666:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1026:- Note-path and note-content policy are also embedded here. That is acceptable for a bridge script, but note schema, status vocabulary, and execution-record structure should eventually be owned by a note/history module rather than the process launcher ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:96), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:187), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:50)).
3667:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1027:- The runner currently decides final state as `SUCCESS` or `FAILED` based only on the subprocess exit code. That is an expedient bridge behavior, but review judgment, validation outcome, and richer state modeling should not stay inside the launcher if the workflow matures ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:141), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:124)).
3668:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1031:- As bridge tooling it is still acceptable, but it is only partially subordinate to workflow. It captures execution, yet it does not encode the review point that should separate execution from acceptance. A zero exit code becomes `SUCCESS` immediately, which skips the workflow's "validate and inspect before the next step" posture ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:271), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:125)).
3669:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1032:- It stores the original prompt text and final output, but not explicit success criteria, validation evidence, or reviewer judgment. That means the note is an execution artifact, not yet a full workflow artifact ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:251), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
3670:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1036:- The current setup provides some raw failure evidence because nonzero exit runs still write a note and stderr is preserved when present ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:343), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:128)).
3671:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1042:- The setup preserves only minimal cost evidence today: timestamp in the note filename/content and the captured final output/stderr. That gives some weak signal about artifact volume, but almost nothing about runtime, retries, or service usage ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:382), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:17), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
3672-notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1085:I have the governing constraints and the main seam: the current bridge runner records execution, but it conflates execution success with reviewed acceptance and leaves the run artifact underdefined. I’m checking the existing prompt queue so the next sequence stays small and fits the repository’s current naming pattern.

3966:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1283:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
3967:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1295:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
3968:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1297:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
3969-notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1299:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
3970-notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1300:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
3971:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1304:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
3972-notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1315:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.

3995:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2028:Built `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` as a thin V1 runner candidate that resolves one prompt, runs `codex exec` from the repo root, captures `--output-last-message`, captures stderr and return code, writes one V1 execution-record markdown file into `notes/`, prints that path, and exits with the subprocess return code.

4001:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2105:`tools/codex/run_prompt.py` appears to satisfy the intended V1 bridge-runner role: it executes one prompt, writes one durable execution record in `notes/`, separates execution from review, and stops at `review_status: UNREVIEWED`. The sample record is structurally close to the V1 artifact shape and preserves the failure evidence that matters. The most important next issue is environment-related, not runner design or broader implementation.
4002:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2109:### Does `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` successfully implement the V1 design intent?

4097:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1273:`tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md` is directionally aligned with the governing workflow because it pushes toward smaller parts, clearer ownership, and conservative growth. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` is acceptable as thin bridge tooling for a single bounded slice, but the current architecture doc does not yet translate the workflow into a clear V1 boundary, validation plan, review loop, or failure/resource evidence model. The next step should stay at the design layer: define the minimum execution-record artifact that every run must produce before more tooling is added.
4098:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1294:- The runner does one bounded thing: resolve a prompt, execute `codex exec`, and write a local note. That fits the workflow's allowance for thin bridge tooling that gathers evidence while remaining inspectable and reversible ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:114), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:99)).
4099:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1295:- It validates basic directory prerequisites and fails cleanly when expected folders or prompt files are missing, which is a reasonable minimal safeguard for a bridge script ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:109), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:108)).
4100:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1296:- It writes timestamped markdown notes into `notes/`, which gives the system at least some durable local history of what was run and what Codex returned ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:120), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:46), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:135)).
4101:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1300:- Prompt resolution policy is embedded in the runner. Selection by absolute path, repo-relative path, `codex_prompts/` path, or globbed prefix is helpful now, but prompt identity and lookup rules belong in a prompt-discovery/status layer if the system grows ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:47), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:25)).
4102:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1301:- Note-path and note-content policy are also embedded here. That is acceptable for a bridge script, but note schema, status vocabulary, and execution-record structure should eventually be owned by a note/history module rather than the process launcher ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:96), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:187), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:50)).
4103:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1302:- The runner currently decides final state as `SUCCESS` or `FAILED` based only on the subprocess exit code. That is an expedient bridge behavior, but review judgment, validation outcome, and richer state modeling should not stay inside the launcher if the workflow matures ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:141), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:124)).
4104:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1306:- As bridge tooling it is still acceptable, but it is only partially subordinate to workflow. It captures execution, yet it does not encode the review point that should separate execution from acceptance. A zero exit code becomes `SUCCESS` immediately, which skips the workflow's "validate and inspect before the next step" posture ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:271), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:125)).
4105:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1307:- It stores the original prompt text and final output, but not explicit success criteria, validation evidence, or reviewer judgment. That means the note is an execution artifact, not yet a full workflow artifact ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:251), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
4106:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1311:- The current setup provides some raw failure evidence because nonzero exit runs still write a note and stderr is preserved when present ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:343), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:128)).
4107:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1317:- The setup preserves only minimal cost evidence today: timestamp in the note filename/content and the captured final output/stderr. That gives some weak signal about artifact volume, but almost nothing about runtime, retries, or service usage ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:382), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:17), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).

4235-notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:849:- it can be created cheaply by the bridge runner
4236-notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1057:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
4237:notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1063:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
4238-notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1080:- a larger queue state machine
4239:notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1126:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
4240:notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1138:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
4241:notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1140:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
4242-notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1142:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
4243-notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1143:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
4244:notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1147:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
4245-notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1158:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
4246-notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1188:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
4247-notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1195:- automatic queue release
4248-notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1197:- separate queue-level state files
4249-notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1205:The minimum V1 review gate is:

4262:notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1702:- execute one prompt through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
4263:notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1713:- Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
4264:notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1714:- Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
4265:notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1715:- Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
4266:notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1716:- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
4267:notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1717:- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).
4268-notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1724:- The helper is consistent with the V1 review gate. It only allows `ACCEPTED` or `REJECTED`, auto-fills `reviewed_at_utc`, and limits failure-analysis updates to rejected runs. See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).

4461-notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:11:This is a primary educational repository for the 3rd edition of "Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras and TensorFlow" (O'Reilly book by Aurélien Géron), containing 19 chapter-structured Jupyter notebooks covering ML fundamentals through reinforcement learning and deployment at scale. 
--

4513-notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:869:### 4. V1 review gate definition
4514-notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:883:No additional required queue file, database, or sidecar should be introduced in V1.
--
4517-notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:950:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
4518-notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:961:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.
4519-notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:997:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
4520:notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1003:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
4521-notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1020:- a larger queue state machine
4522:notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1066:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
4523:notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1078:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
4524:notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1080:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
4525-notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1082:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
4526-notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1083:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
4527:notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1087:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
4528-notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1098:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
4529-notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1128:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
4530-notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1135:- automatic queue release
4531-notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1137:- separate queue-level state files
4532-notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1145:The minimum V1 review gate is:
4533:notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1162:Define the smallest change set for [baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py) so the current bridge runner writes a V1 execution record in `notes/` without a broader refactor.
4534:notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1174:This spec covers only the current bridge runner at [baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py).
4535-notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1199:The current gap is that the note conflates execution completion with outcome labeling and does not initialize the V1 review gate.

4544:notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2446:The V1 prompt workflow is now real, not just specified. The repo has a working runner at `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, an in-place review write-back helper at `tools/codex/review_run.py`, a queue-readiness checker at `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`, and a review-backlog lister at `tools/codex/list_review_backlog.py`.
4545:notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2454:- One bounded prompt run can be executed through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`.

4549-notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2474:  - It still frames review write-back and queue logic as not yet implemented, while the repo now has separate helpers for both.
4550:notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2475:- The execution-record spec says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` now adds same-second suffixes like `__2` when needed. The code is pragmatic; the spec lags.
4551:notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2483:The repo has outgrown parts of the design packet. The next prompt or reviewer could still be steered by stale references to `baby_run_prompt.py`, by the old strict run-id rule, or by the older assumption that readiness/backlog support does not exist.
4552:notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2493:`run_prompt.py`, `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py` all rely on the same markdown field and section conventions. That contract is now central to the workflow, but it is only protected by careful coding and prior notes, not by a small repeatable test layer.

4900:notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:182:Implemented the V1 runner candidate in [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py). It keeps the bootstrap runner’s prompt resolution and thin `codex exec` subprocess flow, but now writes a single V1 execution-record markdown artifact with separate execution and review sections, blank review/failure-analysis fields, lightweight resource metrics, stable run-id naming, and numeric suffix collision handling.
4901:notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:184:I also wrote the implementation note at [009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md) and validated the runner by invoking it against the smoke-test prompt. That produced [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md), which has the required V1 shape. The `codex exec` subprocess itself exited `1` because session initialization hit `Read-only file system (os error 30)`, so the sample record correctly shows `execution_status: EXECUTION_FAILED`, `review_status: UNREVIEWED`, captured stderr, and the printed record path. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` was not modified.

4926-notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:915:- it can be created cheaply by the bridge runner
4927-notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1123:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
4928:notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1129:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
4929-notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1146:- a larger queue state machine
4930:notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1192:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
4931:notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1204:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
4932:notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1206:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
4933-notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1208:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
4934-notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1209:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
4935:notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1213:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
4936-notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1224:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
4937-notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1254:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
4938-notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1261:- automatic queue release
4939-notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1263:- separate queue-level state files

5036:notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:4960:Implemented the V1 runner candidate in [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py). It keeps the bootstrap runner’s prompt resolution and thin `codex exec` subprocess flow, but now writes a single V1 execution-record markdown artifact with separate execution and review sections, blank review/failure-analysis fields, lightweight resource metrics, stable run-id naming, and numeric suffix collision handling.
5037:notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:4962:I also wrote the implementation note at [009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md) and validated the runner by invoking it against the smoke-test prompt. That produced [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md), which has the required V1 shape. The `codex exec` subprocess itself exited `1` because session initialization hit `Read-only file system (os error 30)`, so the sample record correctly shows `execution_status: EXECUTION_FAILED`, `review_status: UNREVIEWED`, captured stderr, and the printed record path. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` was not modified.

5048:notes/010_run_prompt_candidate_review__20260415_234559.md:5:`tools/codex/run_prompt.py` appears to satisfy the intended V1 bridge-runner role: it executes one prompt, writes one durable execution record in `notes/`, separates execution from review, and stops at `review_status: UNREVIEWED`. The sample record is structurally close to the V1 artifact shape and preserves the failure evidence that matters. The most important next issue is environment-related, not runner design or broader implementation.
5049:notes/010_run_prompt_candidate_review__20260415_234559.md:9:### Does `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` successfully implement the V1 design intent?
5050-notes/010_run_prompt_candidate_review__20260415_234559.md:21:- no queue automation or review automation
5051:notes/010_run_prompt_candidate_review__20260415_234559.md:25:- It stays thin and preserves the bounded behavior of `baby_run_prompt.py` instead of turning into a larger system.
5052:notes/010_run_prompt_candidate_review__20260415_234559.md:36:- The runner identity now correctly points at `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, but `tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md` still describes the prior target as `baby_run_prompt.py`. That is a doc drift issue more than a runner issue.
5053:notes/010_run_prompt_candidate_review__20260415_234559.md:107:- Nothing in `run_prompt.py` suggests it is forcing a read-only mode or attempting forbidden writes beyond the existing thin subprocess path.

5131-notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:955:- it can be created cheaply by the bridge runner
5132-notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1152:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
5133:notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1158:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
5134-notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1175:- a larger queue state machine
5135:notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1221:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
5136:notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1233:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
5137:notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1235:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
5138-notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1237:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
5139-notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1238:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
5140:notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1242:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
5141-notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1253:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
5142-notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1283:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
5143-notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1290:- automatic queue release
--

5278:notes/009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md:5:Built `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` as a thin V1 runner candidate that resolves one prompt, runs `codex exec` from the repo root, captures `--output-last-message`, captures stderr and return code, writes one V1 execution-record markdown file into `notes/`, prints that path, and exits with the subprocess return code.
