5292:notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:5:`tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md` is directionally aligned with the governing workflow because it pushes toward smaller parts, clearer ownership, and conservative growth. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` is acceptable as thin bridge tooling for a single bounded slice, but the current architecture doc does not yet translate the workflow into a clear V1 boundary, validation plan, review loop, or failure/resource evidence model. The next step should stay at the design layer: define the minimum execution-record artifact that every run must produce before more tooling is added.
5293:notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:26:- The runner does one bounded thing: resolve a prompt, execute `codex exec`, and write a local note. That fits the workflow's allowance for thin bridge tooling that gathers evidence while remaining inspectable and reversible ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:114), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:99)).
5294:notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:27:- It validates basic directory prerequisites and fails cleanly when expected folders or prompt files are missing, which is a reasonable minimal safeguard for a bridge script ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:109), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:108)).
5295:notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:28:- It writes timestamped markdown notes into `notes/`, which gives the system at least some durable local history of what was run and what Codex returned ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:120), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:46), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:135)).
5296:notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:32:- Prompt resolution policy is embedded in the runner. Selection by absolute path, repo-relative path, `codex_prompts/` path, or globbed prefix is helpful now, but prompt identity and lookup rules belong in a prompt-discovery/status layer if the system grows ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:47), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:25)).
5297:notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:33:- Note-path and note-content policy are also embedded here. That is acceptable for a bridge script, but note schema, status vocabulary, and execution-record structure should eventually be owned by a note/history module rather than the process launcher ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:96), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:187), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:50)).
5298:notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:34:- The runner currently decides final state as `SUCCESS` or `FAILED` based only on the subprocess exit code. That is an expedient bridge behavior, but review judgment, validation outcome, and richer state modeling should not stay inside the launcher if the workflow matures ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:141), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:124)).
5299:notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:38:- As bridge tooling it is still acceptable, but it is only partially subordinate to workflow. It captures execution, yet it does not encode the review point that should separate execution from acceptance. A zero exit code becomes `SUCCESS` immediately, which skips the workflow's "validate and inspect before the next step" posture ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:271), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:125)).
5300:notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:39:- It stores the original prompt text and final output, but not explicit success criteria, validation evidence, or reviewer judgment. That means the note is an execution artifact, not yet a full workflow artifact ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:251), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
5301:notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:43:- The current setup provides some raw failure evidence because nonzero exit runs still write a note and stderr is preserved when present ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:343), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:128)).
5302:notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:49:- The setup preserves only minimal cost evidence today: timestamp in the note filename/content and the captured final output/stderr. That gives some weak signal about artifact volume, but almost nothing about runtime, retries, or service usage ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:382), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:17), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).

5314:notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:139:The recommendation is `environment diagnosis prompt`. The review concludes `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` is close enough to the V1 intent, the sample execution record has the right separation between execution and review, and the read-only failure appears to be primarily a Codex session/environment problem rather than a runner defect.

5332-notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1003:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
5333-notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1014:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.
5334-notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1050:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
5335:notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1056:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
5336-notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1073:- a larger queue state machine
5337:notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1119:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
5338:notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1131:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
5339:notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1133:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
5340-notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1135:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
5341-notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1136:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
5342:notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1140:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
5343-notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1151:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
5344-notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1181:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
5345-notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1188:- automatic queue release
5346-notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1190:- separate queue-level state files

5505:notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:7050:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:2806:The review concludes the V1 pipe is now a real usable thin slice: `run_prompt.py` creates durable execution records, `review_run.py` performs in-place manual review write-back, and the main remaining weakness is operational support for repeated use rather than missing architecture. The top recommendation is to add a small queue-readiness checker next, followed by an unreviewed-run lister and then lightweight contract validation for the two V1 scripts.

6314:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1181:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
6315:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1193:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
6316:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1195:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
6317-notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1197:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
6318-notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1198:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
6319:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1202:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
6320-notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1213:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
6321-notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1243:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.

6494:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4925:`tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md` is directionally aligned with the governing workflow because it pushes toward smaller parts, clearer ownership, and conservative growth. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` is acceptable as thin bridge tooling for a single bounded slice, but the current architecture doc does not yet translate the workflow into a clear V1 boundary, validation plan, review loop, or failure/resource evidence model. The next step should stay at the design layer: define the minimum execution-record artifact that every run must produce before more tooling is added.
6495:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4946:- The runner does one bounded thing: resolve a prompt, execute `codex exec`, and write a local note. That fits the workflow's allowance for thin bridge tooling that gathers evidence while remaining inspectable and reversible ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:114), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:99)).
6496:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4947:- It validates basic directory prerequisites and fails cleanly when expected folders or prompt files are missing, which is a reasonable minimal safeguard for a bridge script ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:109), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:108)).
6497:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4948:- It writes timestamped markdown notes into `notes/`, which gives the system at least some durable local history of what was run and what Codex returned ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:120), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:46), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:135)).
6498:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4952:- Prompt resolution policy is embedded in the runner. Selection by absolute path, repo-relative path, `codex_prompts/` path, or globbed prefix is helpful now, but prompt identity and lookup rules belong in a prompt-discovery/status layer if the system grows ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:47), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:25)).
6499:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4953:- Note-path and note-content policy are also embedded here. That is acceptable for a bridge script, but note schema, status vocabulary, and execution-record structure should eventually be owned by a note/history module rather than the process launcher ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:96), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:187), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:50)).
6500:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4954:- The runner currently decides final state as `SUCCESS` or `FAILED` based only on the subprocess exit code. That is an expedient bridge behavior, but review judgment, validation outcome, and richer state modeling should not stay inside the launcher if the workflow matures ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:141), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:124)).
6501:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4958:- As bridge tooling it is still acceptable, but it is only partially subordinate to workflow. It captures execution, yet it does not encode the review point that should separate execution from acceptance. A zero exit code becomes `SUCCESS` immediately, which skips the workflow's "validate and inspect before the next step" posture ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:271), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:125)).
6502:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4959:- It stores the original prompt text and final output, but not explicit success criteria, validation evidence, or reviewer judgment. That means the note is an execution artifact, not yet a full workflow artifact ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:251), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
6503:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4963:- The current setup provides some raw failure evidence because nonzero exit runs still write a note and stderr is preserved when present ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:343), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:128)).
6504:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4969:- The setup preserves only minimal cost evidence today: timestamp in the note filename/content and the captured final output/stderr. That gives some weak signal about artifact volume, but almost nothing about runtime, retries, or service usage ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:382), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:17), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).

6593:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:6548:Implemented the V1 runner candidate in [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py). It keeps the bootstrap runner’s prompt resolution and thin `codex exec` subprocess flow, but now writes a single V1 execution-record markdown artifact with separate execution and review sections, blank review/failure-analysis fields, lightweight resource metrics, stable run-id naming, and numeric suffix collision handling.
6594:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:6550:I also wrote the implementation note at [009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md) and validated the runner by invoking it against the smoke-test prompt. That produced [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md), which has the required V1 shape. The `codex exec` subprocess itself exited `1` because session initialization hit `Read-only file system (os error 30)`, so the sample record correctly shows `execution_status: EXECUTION_FAILED`, `review_status: UNREVIEWED`, captured stderr, and the printed record path. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` was not modified.

6601:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:6633:Built `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` as a thin V1 runner candidate that resolves one prompt, runs `codex exec` from the repo root, captures `--output-last-message`, captures stderr and return code, writes one V1 execution-record markdown file into `notes/`, prints that path, and exits with the subprocess return code.

6628:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:6984:`tools/codex/run_prompt.py` appears to satisfy the intended V1 bridge-runner role: it executes one prompt, writes one durable execution record in `notes/`, separates execution from review, and stops at `review_status: UNREVIEWED`. The sample record is structurally close to the V1 artifact shape and preserves the failure evidence that matters. The most important next issue is environment-related, not runner design or broader implementation.

6661:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7642:The review concludes the V1 pipe is now a real usable thin slice: `run_prompt.py` creates durable execution records, `review_run.py` performs in-place manual review write-back, and the main remaining weakness is operational support for repeated use rather than missing architecture. The top recommendation is to add a small queue-readiness checker next, followed by an unreviewed-run lister and then lightweight contract validation for the two V1 scripts.

6673:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7789:- Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
6674:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7790:- Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
6675:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7791:- Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
6676:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7792:- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
6677:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7793:- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).
6678-notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7800:- The helper is consistent with the V1 review gate. It only allows `ACCEPTED` or `REJECTED`, auto-fills `reviewed_at_utc`, and limits failure-analysis updates to rejected runs. See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).

6803-notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9211:Validation result: both helpers behave correctly and conservatively against the current repo state. `check_queue_readiness.py` correctly treats `002` as the next ready prompt because only `001` has a latest `ACCEPTED` V1 record, and `list_review_backlog.py` correctly distinguishes the stale unreviewed failed `001` run from the newer accepted `001` run by keeping it in the all-unreviewed list but excluding it from latest-per-prompt and likely-needs-review-next.

6858:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9612:The V1 prompt workflow is now real, not just specified. The repo has a working runner at `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, an in-place review write-back helper at `tools/codex/review_run.py`, a queue-readiness checker at `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`, and a review-backlog lister at `tools/codex/list_review_backlog.py`.
6859:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9620:- One bounded prompt run can be executed through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`.
6860-notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9624:- Conservative queue progression is operational through `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`.
6861:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9638:  - It still targets `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`.
6862:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9639:  - It still names the runner field as `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`.
6863-notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9640:  - It still frames review write-back and queue logic as not yet implemented, while the repo now has separate helpers for both.
6864:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9641:- The execution-record spec says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` now adds same-second suffixes like `__2` when needed. The code is pragmatic; the spec lags.
6865:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9649:The repo has outgrown parts of the design packet. The next prompt or reviewer could still be steered by stale references to `baby_run_prompt.py`, by the old strict run-id rule, or by the older assumption that readiness/backlog support does not exist.
6866:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9659:`run_prompt.py`, `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py` all rely on the same markdown field and section conventions. That contract is now central to the workflow, but it is only protected by careful coding and prior notes, not by a small repeatable test layer.
6867-notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9662:- repeated required-section and required-field parsing logic in `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py`
6868-notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9672:That is not a V1 architecture failure, but it does create friction when interpreting readiness and when deciding whether to trust the queue as a day-to-day operational surface.

6969:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:10918:  `tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md` is directionally aligned with the governing workflow because it pushes toward smaller parts, clearer ownership, and conservative growth. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` is acceptable as thin bridge tooling for a single bounded slice, but the current architecture doc does not yet translate the workflow into a clear V1 boundary, validation plan, review loop, or failure/resource evidence model. The next step should stay at the design layer: define the minimum execution-record artifact that every run must produce before more tooling is added.

7000:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11215:  `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` appears to satisfy the intended V1 bridge-runner role: it executes one prompt, writes one durable execution record in `notes/`, separates execution from review, and stops at `review_status: UNREVIEWED`. The sample record is structurally close to the V1 artifact shape and preserves the failure evidence that matters. The most important next issue is environment-related, not runner design or broader implementation.

7008:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11360:  - Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
7009:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11361:  - Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
7010:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11362:  - Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
7011:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11363:  - Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
7012:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11364:  - The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).

7103:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12335:The review concludes the V1 pipe is now a real usable thin slice: `run_prompt.py` creates durable execution records, `review_run.py` performs in-place manual review write-back, and the main remaining weakness is operational support for repeated use rather than missing architecture. 7131:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12594:- The execution-record spec says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` now adds same-second suffixes like `__2` when needed. The code is pragmatic; the spec lags.
7132:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12602:The repo has outgrown parts of the design packet. The next prompt or reviewer could still be steered by stale references to `baby_run_prompt.py`, by the old strict run-id rule, or by the older assumption that readiness/backlog support does not exist.
7133:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12612:`run_prompt.py`, `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py` all rely on the same markdown field and section conventions. That contract is now central to the workflow, but it is only protected by careful coding and prior notes, not by a small repeatable test layer.

7135-notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12625:That is not a V1 architecture failure, but it does create friction when interpreting readiness and when deciding whether to trust the queue as a day-to-day operational surface.

7387:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:97:- Do not expand queue semantics, retry tooling, or orchestration behavior
7388:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:107:3. Confirm `run_prompt.py` still writes records that the shared validator accepts.
7389-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:108:4. Confirm `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py` still work with the shared validator in place.
7390:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:124:Implemented a shared V1 record validator in [v1_record_validation.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/v1_record_validation.py) and rewired [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py), [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py), [check_queue_readiness.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py), and [list_review_backlog.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/list_review_backlog.py) to reuse that one path instead of carrying separate markdown-contract checks. The shared path now enforces required section order, required field lines, allowed status values, the `run_id` pattern with optional same-second suffix, and basic identity consistency such as title/run-id and prompt-file/prompt-stem agreement.
7391:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:129:- a stubbed `run_prompt.py` execution proving it still writes records the shared validator accepts

7393:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:168:- `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
7394-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:170:- `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`

7400-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:241:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
7401:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:247:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
7402-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:264:- a larger queue state machine
7403:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:310:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
7404:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:322:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
7405:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:324:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
7406-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:326:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
7407-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:327:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
7408:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:331:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
7409-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:342:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
7410-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:372:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
7411-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:379:- automatic queue release
--
7413-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:389:The minimum V1 review gate is:
7414-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:446:`005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__20260415_202557`
7415-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:453:- it can be created cheaply by the bridge runner
7416:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:455:`tools/codex/run_prompt.py` uses that base identity and adds a numeric suffix such as `__2` only when needed to avoid a same-second filename collision for the same prompt.
7417:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:624:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' tools/codex/run_prompt.py" in /data/git/handson-ml3
7418:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:641:RUNNER_PATH = "tools/codex/run_prompt.py"
7419-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1056:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py" in /data/git/handson-ml3
7420-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1136:        description="Report whether the next prompt is ready under the current V1 review gate."
7421:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1590:The V1 prompt workflow is now real, not just specified. The repo has a working runner at `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, an in-place review write-back helper at `tools/codex/review_run.py`, a queue-readiness checker at `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`, and a review-backlog lister at `tools/codex/list_review_backlog.py`.
7422:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1598:- One bounded prompt run can be executed through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`.
7423-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1602:- Conservative queue progression is operational through `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`.
7424:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1616:  - It still targets `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`.
7425:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1617:  - It still names the runner field as `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`.
7426-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1618:  - It still frames review write-back and queue logic as not yet implemented, while the repo now has separate helpers for both.
7427:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1619:- The execution-record spec says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` now adds same-second suffixes like `__2` when needed. The code is pragmatic; the spec lags.
7428:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1627:The repo has outgrown parts of the design packet. The next prompt or reviewer could still be steered by stale references to `baby_run_prompt.py`, by the old strict run-id rule, or by the older assumption that readiness/backlog support does not exist.
7429:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1637:`run_prompt.py`, `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py` all rely on the same markdown field and section conventions. That contract is now central to the workflow, but it is only protected by careful coding and prior notes, not by a small repeatable test layer.
7430-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1640:- repeated required-section and required-field parsing logic in `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py`
7431-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1650:That is not a V1 architecture failure, but it does create friction when interpreting readiness and when deciding whether to trust the queue as a day-to-day operational surface.
7432-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1653:- `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` needed a special explanatory `Queue note:`
7433-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1656:### 4. Defer retry-linkage tooling and richer queue semantics
7434-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1685:4. `Retry-linkage helper or richer queue semantics`
7435:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1702:- update the bridge-runner spec to reflect `run_prompt.py`
7436:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1717:- The repo now has `run_prompt.py`, `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py`, but parts of the design packet still describe the older bridge-runner state and strict unsuffixed run ids.
7437-notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:1730:- richer queue states or scheduling

7978:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:28593:Implemented a shared V1 record validator in [v1_record_validation.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/v1_record_validation.py) and rewired [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py), [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py), [check_queue_readiness.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py), and [list_review_backlog.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/list_review_backlog.py) to reuse that one path instead of carrying separate markdown-contract checks. The shared path now enforces required section order, required field lines, allowed status values, the `run_id` pattern with optional same-second suffix, and basic identity consistency such as title/run-id and prompt-file/prompt-stem agreement.
7979:notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:28598:- a stubbed `run_prompt.py` execution proving it still writes records the shared validator accepts

You are working inside this repository.

Your job is NOT to implement the notebook wrapper yet.

Your job is to:
1. inspect the existing Codex runner tooling and related design notes,
2. assess how a staged notebook-treatment wrapper could be built around the current Codex-centered workflow,
3. write a clear implementation-planning report into `notes/`,
4. make no code changes.

## Core intent

We want to keep the workflow centered around Codex calls.

The desired future behavior is a wrapper that can launch one narrow Codex task at a time against a notebook set, instead of asking Codex to solve the entire notebook in one pass.

2026-04-17T17:34:08.393015Z ERROR codex_core::tools::router: error=exec_command failed for `/bin/bash -lc "cat > notes/025_first_prompt_audit__20260417_123322.md <<'EOF'
# First Prompt Audit Report

## Executive Summary
The proposed first wrapper-build prompt should stay as an MVP-contract prompt, but its scope needs tightening. It is the right first move only if it defines the wrapper boundary and invariants that later build prompts must preserve, while deferring detailed resume mechanics, scan logic, and orchestration implementation to later prompts.

## Evidence Reviewed
- "'`notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md`
- `notes/024_generate_staged_notebook_wrapper_prompts__20260417_172452.md`
- `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
- `tools/codex/review_run.py`
- `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`
- `tools/codex/list_review_backlog.py`
- `tools/codex/v1_record_validation.py`
- `tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md`
- `tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md`
- `tools/notebook_enricher/notebook_scanner.py`
- `tools/notebook_enricher/prompt_builder.py`
- `codex_prompts/023_tools_codex_assessment.md`
- `codex_prompts/024_generate_staged_notebook_wrapper_prompts.md`
- `codex_prompts/025_audit_025_mvp_contract_prompt_against_assessment.md`

## What This First Prompt Should Cover
The first prompt should define the wrapper contract, not the wrapper implementation. In this repo that means it should fence the MVP around a thin layer that stays outside `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, uses the existing V1 note-and-review workflow unchanged, and treats notebook mutation as a later controlled step rather than an immediate build goal.

It should explicitly cover the MVP boundary, runner-centered integration rule, durable artifacts the wrapper may introduce outside the V1 execution record, the minimum scope-input shape for selecting notebooks and stages, and the top-level state model only at the level needed to prevent drift. That state model should be coarse: target notebook set, active stage, next work item identity, completion marker semantics, and stop conditions. It should also define non-goals, especially no runner redesign, no V1 record format change, no direct `codex exec` bypass, no broad queue engine, and no notebook-wide enrichment pass.

What is absolutely required in this first prompt is the invariant set later prompts must obey. The key invariants are: wrapper calls remain centered on `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`; V1 records in `notes/` remain the execution/review source of truth for each prompt run; wrapper-owned progress tracking must be additive rather than a replacement for V1 records; notebook writes must be narrow and reviewable; and stop/resume must be safe rather than optimistic.

## What This First Prompt Should Not Cover
This first prompt should not define the detailed resume mechanism, exact progress-file schema, scan-report schema, notebook mutation algorithm, prompt-generation templates for stages 1 to 3, or the orchestration loop itself. Those are later build concerns and the 024 plan already split them into 026 through 030.

It also should not import too much from `tools/notebook_enricher/prompt_builder.py`. That file is implementation-heavy and assumes direct notebook rewriting to `.tmp` outputs. The first wrapper prompt should not lock the new design to those concrete write-path details before the repo has agreed on wrapper-owned artifacts and safety boundaries.

Putting full wrapper state model details, scope/config inputs, reporting artifacts, and non-goals into 025 is acceptable only if they stay at contract level. If “wrapper state model” means full persisted schema and resume behavior, that is too much for one prompt and belongs in 026. If “reporting artifacts” means exact per-run report formats beyond naming the artifact classes and their role, that is also premature.

## Alignment With The Wrapper Assessment
This revised 025 concept aligns with the earlier assessment if it stays runner-centered. The assessment question set explicitly pushed toward a thin orchestration layer that emits prompt files and calls `run_prompt.py` repeatedly, and the current V1 tooling is built around one prompt, one record, one review gate. `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py` all assume that bounded shape.

It also aligns with stop/resume concerns, but only at boundary level. The assessment asked how progress and idempotency could be tracked and when it is safe to stop or resume. A first prompt should define that those concerns are mandatory and additive to the current V1 record flow, not settle every persistence detail immediately.

It aligns with notebook mutation safety if it makes safety a governing constraint before any scan or write code is built. That matters because `tools/notebook_enricher/notebook_scanner.py` is read-only analysis, while `tools/notebook_enricher/prompt_builder.py` assumes direct notebook rewrite outputs. The first wrapper prompt should preserve the repo'"'s MVP-first discipline by deciding that mutation rules are constrained by contract first and implemented later.

It aligns with thin-slice sequencing if it remains a scope-and-invariants prompt. It stops aligning if it expands into detailed state persistence or scan output design, because that would collapse 025 and 026 together and weaken the staged build plan generated in 024.

## Risks If This Prompt Is Too Broad Or Too Thin
If 025 is too broad, it will pre-decide persisted wrapper schema, resume mechanics, reporting formats, and scan behavior before the repo has validated the narrower contract. That creates a risk of pseudo-architecture work that is out of proportion to the current V1 runner design, which is intentionally conservative and single-run.

If 025 is too thin, later prompts can drift into incompatible assumptions. The main drift risks are bypassing "'`run_prompt.py`, treating wrapper progress files as a replacement for V1 records, overfitting to the old notebook enricher'"'s "'`.tmp` rewrite pattern, or quietly broadening the MVP into a multi-stage automation engine before stage-specific prompt safety is defined.

## Verdict
REVISE

The proposed first prompt is directionally correct, but the current description is slightly overloaded. “MVP boundary” and “non-goals” clearly belong in 025. A contract-level definition of wrapper-owned artifact classes and coarse state concepts also belongs there. But detailed state/resume design and detailed reporting artifacts belong in later prompts, especially 026 and 027. So the right move is to keep 025 as the first prompt, but revise its scope from “define the contract plus several concrete subsystems” to “define the boundary, invariants, and deferred decisions that govern the later subsystem prompts.”

## Recommendation For The Next Step
Revise the idea for 025 first, then write the actual 025 prompt. The prompt should ask for a repo-specific MVP contract that fixes the boundary, invariants, minimum required artifact classes, coarse scope-input concepts, and explicit deferrals. After that, 026 can safely define the actual scope/state/resume contract without reopening first-principles questions.
